content: Sensory-friendly game audio: limited sound count, predictable/consistent cues, calm simple melodies with little build-up, no sudden or unpredictable loud sounds (aversion to unpredictable sounds is prevalent in auditory-sensitive kids). Audio should follow the player's situation (uplifting on accomplishment) and avoid intrusive repetition. Repetitive gentle actions/sounds can be calming. Sources: Springer lit review on sounds for autistic players, educational-games.online, arXiv educational game design.
